query:
after delivery... sec review findings: Finding 1 — Phase 8 cap-break ordering dependency (low, non-blocking)

Location: nli_detection_tick.rs:594-596

Phase 8 iterates the full merged_pairs vec (which contains both SupportsContradict and Informs variants) with a cap break. The break is safe because Phase 6 appends Supports pairs first and Informs pairs second, so the break only fires within the Supports block. However, this ordering invariant is implicit — it lives in Phase 6's fetch order, not at the break site.

Recommendation: Add a comment at the Phase 8 break noting the ordering dependency: "Informs variants appear after SupportsContradict in merged_pairs (Phase 6 fetch order). Break only fires within Supports block." This prevents a future Phase 6 ordering change from silently causing Phase 8b to miss Informs variants. Not blocking.

Finding 2 — debug_assert for weight finitude (low, non-blocking)

Location: nli_detection_tick.rs:642

debug_assert!(weight.is_finite()) is elided in release builds. Input constraints (cosine in (-1,1), ppr_weight in [0,1] validated at startup) make non-finite weight practically impossible. Consider a belt-and-suspenders if !weight.is_finite() { continue; } guard before write_nli_edge to keep production consistent with debug. Not blocking.

entries.title: ADR crt-037 (replacement): Independent per-type budgets in nli_detection_tick Phase 5
Shared tick cap starves low-priority candidate type when high-priority fills the budget
Use pre-cloned lock snapshot for new pipeline steps — never re-acquire the lock
